title: RAG retrieval-mode policy: typed RetrievalMode enum + per-operation routing (folded WP-1-RAG-Retrieval-Mode-Policy)
goal: Define and implement one governed retrieval-mode policy so QueryPlan, RetrievalTrace, Project Brain, Prompt-to-Spec Router, Work Packet loads, and Micro-Task context assembly each choose the cheapest authoritative retrieval path instead of defaulting to hybrid retrieval. Folded from WP-1-RAG-Retrieval-Mode-Policy.
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/memory/retrieval_mode.rs (RetrievalMode enum + RetrievalModePolicy + RetrievalModeRouter types + RoutingRule). Tests src/backend/handshake_core/tests/retrieval_mode_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-141/142/143 ship capsule primitives + TaskType + CapsuleBuilder.
implementation_notes: Per WP-1-RAG-Retrieval-Mode-Policy preserved_intent: 'Handshake now has rich retrieval substrates and graph signals, but it still needs explicit no-RAG and lower-cost retrieval rules so exact identifiers, authoritative packet state, bounded local-small-model loops, and freshness-sensitive operations do not get blurred into one generic semantic-search path'. RetrievalMode enum: `pub enum RetrievalMode { NoRag (use the canonical key/id lookup), FullText, Vector, Hybrid, GraphAware, AuthoritativeOnly (read directly from JSON authority file) }`. RoutingRule: `{ id: String, predicate: Box<dyn Fn(&RetrievalContext) -> bool>, mode: RetrievalMode, rationale: String }`. RetrievalModePolicy: `pub struct RetrievalModePolicy { rules: Vec<RoutingRule>, default_mode: RetrievalMode }`. RetrievalModeRouter: `pub fn route(ctx: &RetrievalContext) -> (RetrievalMode, String) { /* return mode + matched rule id or 'default' */ }`. Default policy rules (initial set): (1) exact_uuid_in_query -> NoRag; (2) wp_id_pattern -> AuthoritativeOnly (load packet.json directly); (3) hbr_id_pattern -> AuthoritativeOnly (load HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json directly); (4) spec_anchor_pattern -> AuthoritativeOnly (load spec module); (5) freshness_sensitive task_type -> Vector + outcome-weighted; (6) general_freeform_query -> Hybrid. CapsuleBuilder (MT-143) MUST consult RetrievalModeRouter before dispatching to retrieval; if NoRag or AuthoritativeOnly, skip embedding lookup entirely and return the typed authoritative item. Tests: every default rule routes correctly to its mode; default applies when no rule matches; router is deterministic; CapsuleBuilder honors the routed mode in fixtures.
risk_if_missed: Without a typed retrieval mode policy every operation defaults to expensive hybrid retrieval; exact-id queries become semantic-search guessing.
validator_focus: RetrievalMode is closed enum; routing is deterministic; default policy rules cover the documented operation classes; CapsuleBuilder consults the router.

implementation_record.summary: MT-164 RetrievalMode + RetrievalModePolicy + RetrievalModeRouter is on disk (src/memory/retrieval_mode.rs, 225 lines, prior session) with 7 inline #[cfg(test)] tests already covering per-rule happy paths. This session added the integration test file tests/retrieval_mode_tests.rs (16 tests) that the contract owned_files names, covering: first-match-wins ordering proof (uuid query inside wp-id query routes to NoRag), fall-through to default Hybrid, partial-uuid + lowercase-wp + 2-digit-hbr false-positive guards, every OperationClass resolves without panic, serde round-trip for every RetrievalMode and OperationClass variant, default policy declares >= 6 rules with unique ids and named rule ids present, AuthoritativeOnly rules have non-trivial rationale, deterministic routing across 16 invocations.

spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_1_no_deferred_live_escape: PASS — retrieval_mode.rs is complete (no '*Unavailable' / 'pending MT-NNN' / 'deferred to follow-on' patterns).
spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_2_external_resource_touch: PASS — integration tests exercise the public RetrievalModeRouter API with real RetrievalModePolicy::default_v0() rules; serde round-trip uses real serde_json.
spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_3_implementer_cannot_self_certi: PASS — implementation prior session; tests this session; both differ from validator role.

red_team_coverage.retrieval_mode_is_closed_enum: covered — mt164_retrieval_mode_round_trips_serde_for_every_variant enumerates all 6 variants; widening requires a typed code change that this test catches via missing variant.
red_team_coverage.authoritative_only_rules_read_json_autho: PARTIAL — the integration test asserts the router returns RetrievalMode::AuthoritativeOnly for the documented patterns AND verifies the rationale strings explicitly name the authority files ("wp_id maps directly to packet.json", "hbr_id maps directly to HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json", "spec anchor reads directly from spec module file"). The actual file-read enforcement (cannot-fall-back-to-vector-search) is the contract of the downstream RAG client that consumes the route result; MT-164 declares the route policy, not the consumer. Validator should verify the CapsuleBuilder (MT-143) honors AuthoritativeOnly by skipping embedding lookup.
red_team_coverage.router_is_deterministic: covered — mt164_router_is_deterministic_across_repeated_invocations runs the same query 16 times and asserts every result matches baseline; mt164_first_match_wins_when_uuid_appears_inside_wp_query additionally pins the rule-iteration order.

residual_risks: AuthoritativeOnly enforcement is split across MT-164 (policy declares the mode) and MT-143/CapsuleBuilder (consumer honors the mode). MT-164 cannot fully guarantee the cannot-fall-back invariant alone.

Regex patterns (spec_re, wp_re, hbr_re, uuid_re) are tested for documented happy/edge cases but not exhaustively fuzzed. A future hardening MT could property-test against the operator's actual query corpus.
Default policy rule set is at minimum 6; a future addition of rules above 6 will not trip the >= 6 guard. If exactly-6 is desired, change the assertion to ==.
No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-164.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: RetrievalMode is a closed enum; widening requires a typed code change.
AuthoritativeOnly rules read JSON authority files directly; cannot fall back to vector search.
Router is deterministic; same context always routes to same mode.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: RetrievalMode is a closed enum (NoRag, FullText, Vector, Hybrid, GraphAware, AuthoritativeOnly). RetrievalModePolicy::default_v0 ships 6 routing rules covering uuid/wp/hbr/spec patterns plus operation-class fallbacks. Router is deterministic (first-match-wins; rule iteration order pinned). 16 + 7 tests pass per builder. PARTIAL coverage of the 'AuthoritativeOnly cannot fall back to vector search' invariant: MT-164 declares the route policy; the actual cannot-fall-back enforcement is downstream in CapsuleBuilder (MT-143). Builder evidence honestly flags this split. Per-MT scope passes, but the cross-MT contract requires MT-143 inspection for full assurance — outside this sub-reviewer's MT set.
